response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and is currently doing AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work, which matches the AI-native environment well. However, Python — a must-have — does not appear anywhere in his skill set or work history (his systems work was in DLang, product work in TypeScript/Node), and evidence of staff-level technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination, and operational/on-call ownership is thin. Worth a conversation if Python can be validated, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Strong TypeScript/Node depth with broad full-stack range and a demonstrated testing/craftsmanship culture
concerns: No Python listed despite it being a must-have; core systems work was in DLang
Limited evidence of directing technical strategy at scale or managing stakeholders across functions
High-throughput experience is dated (2018-2020), recent roles are smaller-scale product work, and there is an unexplained ~8-month gap in 2020
